name: Atiyya al-Awfi
title: Arbaeen Companion
shortDescription: A great Shia Quranic exegete who was present alongside Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Ansari in the first Arbaeen pilgrimage.
fullStory:
Atiyya al-Awfi was a sincere Shia from Kufa, a Quranic exegete, and a great narrator of Hadith. Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) had chosen his name at the time of his birth.

After the event of Karbala, Atiyya, who could not be present on Ashura due to the oppressive conditions, accompanied Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Ansari (the old and blind companion of the Prophet) on a pilgrimage journey to Karbala.

On the 20th of Safar (Arbaeen), he took Jabir to the Euphrates to perform Ghusl, then took his hand and placed it on the soil of Imam Hussain's (AS) holy grave. Atiyya is the main narrator of all the events and words of Jabir during this historic pilgrimage.

After the pilgrimage ended, Atiyya narrates that the caravan of the Ahl al-Bayt (AS) captives, along with Imam Sajjad (AS) and Lady Zaynab (SA), arrived in Karbala from Sham and met them. Atiyya's role in preserving and recording this event is unparalleled.
quoteArabic: فَلَمّا أَنْدَلَيْتُ بِهِ عَلَى القَبْرِ خَرَّ عَلى القَبْرِ مَغْشِيّاً عَلَيْهِ...
quote: (Atiyya's narration:) So when I placed his hand on the grave, he fell unconscious upon it...
